# Page 4^page=4
What is Sharding? 200k S1 Which database server is 5FTOJ in? Server 3! 200k S2 200k S3 200k S4 200k S5 SELECT URL FROM URL_TABLE WHERE URLID = “5FTOJ“ Split 1 million rows table into 5 database instances.. Same schema

# Page 5^page=5
Consistent Hashing postgres:5432 postgres:5433postgres:5434 Hash(“Input1”) 5432 Hash(“Input2”) 5433 Hash(“Input3”) 5434

# Page 6^page=6
Consistent Hashing postgres:5432 postgres:5433postgres:5434 Hash(“Input2”) 5433 Hash(“Input3”) 5434

# Page 7^page=7
Consistent Hashing postgres:5432 postgres:5433postgres:5434 num(“Input2”) % 3 1 +5432 =5433

# Page 8^page=8
Horizontal Partitioning vs Sharding ● HP splits big table into multiple tables in the same database ● Sharding splits big table into multiple tables across multiple database servers ● HP table name changes (or schema) ● Sharding everything is the same but server changes

# Page 9^page=9
Example Code with Postgres (Url shortener) ● Spin up 3 postgres instances with identical schema ○ 5432, 5433, 5434 ● Write to the sharded databases. ● Reads from the sharded databases.

# Page 10^page=10
Pros of Sharding ● Scalability ○ Data ○ Memory ● Security (users can access certain shards) ● Optimal and Smaller index size

# Page 11^page=11
Cons of Sharding ● Complex client (aware of the shard) ● Transactions across shards problem ● Rollbacks ● Schema changes are hard ● Joins ● Has to be something you know in the query

# Page 12^page=12
Summary ● What is sharding? ● Consistent Hashing ● Horizontal Partitioning vs Sharding ● Example (Code with Postgres) ● Pros & Cons
